triumphs.description: Complete your first Copilot session with a knowledge-store entry
triumphs.flavor: The Traveler's light is new. So are you.
triumphs.name: FIRST LIGHT

triumphs.description: Complete a full TDD red→green→refactor cycle using tdd-red, tdd-green, tdd-refactor agents
triumphs.flavor: You did not build before you broke.
triumphs.name: RED BEFORE GREEN

triumphs.description: Pop the blueprint-mode super: complete a full 6-phase Blueprint Mode workflow with all gates honored
triumphs.flavor: The crater is the spec.
triumphs.name: THUNDERCRASH LANDING

triumphs.description: Use the workflow skill correctly in 5 consecutive sessions without scope creep
triumphs.flavor: It doesn't ask for recognition. It shows up.
triumphs.name: TRUSTEE'S OATH

triumphs.description: Use blueprint-mode correctly for 3 separate features — each invocation must pass all planning gates before implementation begins, with gate output documented in the PR description
triumphs.flavor: The plan is not the map. The plan is the weapon.
triumphs.name: ARCHITECT'S BLUEPRINT

triumphs.description: Achieve 10 consecutive GitHub Actions runs that pass on the first attempt — no re-runs, no workflow_dispatch retries, no force-push resets across any repo
triumphs.flavor: The Traveler's Light does not flicker. Neither does your pipeline.
triumphs.name: GREEN UNBROKEN

triumphs.description: Dispatch 3 or more agents simultaneously in a single session — each completing a distinct, real task in parallel — and synthesize their outputs into a unified deliverable
triumphs.flavor: The Vanguard does not take turns. Neither should you.
triumphs.name: COORDINATED FIRE

triumphs.description: Observe a workflow friction point during a session and file a GitHub issue or SKILL.md update to address it before the session ends — the fix is documented, not deferred
triumphs.flavor: The log remembers what the Guardian forgets. Write it down.
triumphs.name: FRICTION LOGGED

triumphs.description: Store your first knowledge-store entry
triumphs.flavor: Every flame starts with a single spark.
triumphs.name: THE CHRONICLE BEGINS

triumphs.description: Accumulate 10 knowledge entries (SYNTHESIS ≥ 10)
triumphs.flavor: Memory has gravity. Enough of it, and even light bends toward it.
triumphs.name: THE LIBRARY

triumphs.description: Pop the principal-software-engineer super on a real architectural decision and implement its recommendations
triumphs.flavor: You did not guess where the fault was. You knew. The hammer agreed.
triumphs.name: HAMMER THROWN

triumphs.description: Ship a merged PR to any open source project using Copilot assistance
triumphs.flavor: The Solar Guardian does not hoard light — they emit it.
triumphs.name: TORCH PASSED

triumphs.description: Contribute a merged PR to any CNCF project repository — governance docs, people site, projects site, endusers site, or landscape data — using the cncf-dev or cncf-community skill
triumphs.flavor: The cloud is not a place. It is a promise made between builders. You kept it.
triumphs.name: THE CNCF PLEDGE

triumphs.description: Publish or substantially update a blog post, documentation page, or user guide — PR merged to bluefin-docs or any public-facing documentation repo
triumphs.flavor: The Light was never meant to be kept. Someone had to write it down.
triumphs.name: WORDS IN THE ARCHIVE

triumphs.description: Record the first Level: increment in any skill's SKILL.md — the moment a skill crosses from loaded to mastered, marked in the permanent record
triumphs.flavor: The Ghost watched the number change. So did you. Neither of you said anything. Both of you knew.
triumphs.name: POWER ASCENDING

triumphs.description: In a single session, meaningfully apply 3 or more distinct skill families — each must produce a real output (PR, knowledge entry, or merged change). Domains genuinely integrated, not sequential
triumphs.flavor: Ikora Rey does not specialize. She contains multitudes. So does the session log.
triumphs.name: THE CONVERGENCE

triumphs.description: Create a new skill file (SKILL.md) and add it to the INDEX.md
triumphs.flavor: You cannot fire what you have not forged.
triumphs.name: THE FORGE

triumphs.description: Pop the se-security-reviewer super and remediate at least one finding
triumphs.flavor: The bubble. Nothing ships without passing through.
triumphs.name: WARD OF DAWN

triumphs.description: Use skill-improver to improve an existing SKILL.md with measurable changes
triumphs.flavor: The weapon is better because you used it.
triumphs.name: RESONANCE UNLOCKED

triumphs.description: Use se-security-reviewer to identify a real security vulnerability — OWASP Top 10 or equivalent — and remediate it with a merged PR. Finding must be actionable, not theoretical
triumphs.flavor: The Void does not hide what you refuse to see. It only waits.
triumphs.name: SHADOW BANISHED

triumphs.description: Complete the full TDD red->green->refactor cycle using all three agents (tdd-red, tdd-green, tdd-refactor) for a real feature — all three phases in a single session, all tests green at the end
triumphs.flavor: Three agents. One truth. The code that emerged was not written. It was proven.
triumphs.name: THE TRIUNE PATH

triumphs.description: Run the doublecheck agent on 3 distinct AI-generated outputs in a season — each must complete all three pipeline layers (self-audit, source verification, adversarial review) and produce a structured report
triumphs.flavor: The first read is for understanding. The second read is for the truth.
triumphs.name: SECOND SIGHT

triumphs.description: Invoke the principal-software-engineer agent on a real architectural decision — the consultation must surface at minimum one design concern or trade-off that materially changes your approach
triumphs.flavor: Zavala does not advise lightly. Neither does the agent. Listen both times.
triumphs.name: VOICE FROM THE TOWER

triumphs.description: Ship a new Homebrew cask or formula using the homebrew-tap skills
triumphs.flavor: The first thread. The mesh begins.
triumphs.name: FIRST WEAVE

triumphs.description: Pop the github-actions-expert super to build a complete GH Actions workflow
triumphs.flavor: Thread by thread. System by system. The Weave does not negotiate with entropy.
triumphs.name: BLADEFURY ACTIVATED

triumphs.description: Successfully distribute software to users via 3 different mechanisms (brew, flatpak, OCI) in a single season
triumphs.flavor: You didn't pull one thread. You pulled them all. And the mesh held.
triumphs.name: THE WEAVE HOLDS

triumphs.description: Publish a Flatpak application using the flatpak skill — AppStream metainfo passes appstreamcli validate with zero warnings and zero errors, and the submission reaches Flathub or a public remote
triumphs.flavor: The sandbox was not a prison. It was a promise that the app would behave. It did.
triumphs.name: FLATTENED AND SHIPPED

triumphs.description: Submit a Homebrew cask or formula using the homebrew-tap skill that is merged to ublue-os/homebrew-tap or castrojo/tap — CI must pass on the first push
triumphs.flavor: The tap is a contract. The cask is the handshake. Both have been kept.
triumphs.name: THE CASK LANDS

triumphs.description: Build, tag, and push an OCI container image using the oci-images skill — cosign signature verification must pass, and the image must be pullable from GHCR by a separate environment
triumphs.flavor: The image is not a file. It is a promise, signed in cryptographic light.
triumphs.name: SEALED IN LIGHT

triumphs.description: Package the same software in 3 or more distinct distribution formats (e.g., Homebrew cask, Flatpak, OCI image, COPR RPM) across a single season — each format must be independently installable
triumphs.flavor: One target. Three trajectories. The Guardian who can only aim one way is not a threat.
triumphs.name: THREE VECTORS

triumphs.description: Successfully build a Bluefin OCI image using bluefin-build skill
triumphs.flavor: Not everything must move. Some things must simply hold.
triumphs.name: THE CRYSTAL HOLDS

triumphs.description: Pop the swe-subagent super to stabilize a chaotic multi-file implementation
triumphs.flavor: Chaos does not survive the cold. Neither does the bug.
triumphs.name: GLACIAL QUAKE

triumphs.description: Achieve a fully green Bluefin image build — all variant matrix jobs pass, all cosign checks succeed, no CI failures across any matrix dimension
triumphs.flavor: The Behemoth does not bend. It holds. When it holds, nothing moves.
triumphs.name: THE MATRIX HOLDS

triumphs.description: Complete a full season of platform work with zero hotfix commits — all changes go through the proper PR gate, no emergency bypasses, no direct pushes to protected branches
triumphs.flavor: The fortress was not built to be patched. It was built to hold. It held.
triumphs.name: IMMUTABLE COVENANT

triumphs.description: Successfully execute a bootc switch to a locally-built OCI image in a QEMU VM, verify the running system matches the expected image digest, and document the process in the knowledge store
triumphs.flavor: The old system did not die. It became something better. That is the nature of immutable light.
triumphs.name: THE SWITCH IS MADE

triumphs.description: Complete a full bluefin-lts PR gate cycle from start to finish: PR triggers create-lts-pr.yml gate, draft created, squash-merge approved, merged without force pushes or manual interventions
triumphs.flavor: The Queen's Gate is not a formality. It is the proof that the platform was ready.
triumphs.name: GATED AND RELEASED

triumphs.description: Launch castrojo.github.io/powerlevel for the first time
triumphs.flavor: The Guardian turns the tools on themselves.
triumphs.name: THE MIRROR IS FORGED

triumphs.flavor: The trigger stopped surprising you.
triumphs.name: FIRST LIGHT ASCENDING

triumphs.description: Reach ENDURANCE 50 — fifty completed Copilot sessions
triumphs.flavor: Fifty sessions. The Traveler noticed.
triumphs.name: FIFTY LIGHTS

triumphs.description: Witness the first successful pl-gha-compute GitHub Actions run — the workflow that reads SKILL.md Level: fields and writes powerlevel-data.json — all steps green, data updated
triumphs.flavor: The system computed itself. The Traveler watched. The log said: success.
triumphs.name: THE COMPUTATION RUNS

triumphs.description: Reach 50% progress on any seal that has never previously been at 50% — the moment the title shifts from aspiration to trajectory
triumphs.flavor: Halfway is not a destination. It is the proof that the destination exists.
triumphs.name: THE SEAL TURNS

triumphs.description: Cross a Powerlevel milestone threshold (500, 750, 1000, 1250, 1500, or 1750) for the first time — recorded automatically when the dashboard computes the new level
triumphs.flavor: The number changed. The Guardian did not. But something about the room felt different.
triumphs.name: THE THRESHOLD CROSSED

triumphs.description: Complete the BLACKSMITH seal — forge at least one merged upstream Bluefin PR using bluefin-build skill
triumphs.flavor: The forge is behind you. The Blacksmith remembers.
triumphs.name: IRON TEMPERED

triumphs.description: Complete the CURSEBREAKER seal — prove that the loop always holds and friction is never forgotten.
triumphs.flavor: The pipeline runs clean. The loop holds. The mark is yours.
triumphs.name: BREAKER'S MARK

triumphs.description: Complete the DREDGEN seal — identify a real vulnerability with se-security-reviewer and remediate it
triumphs.flavor: You looked into the darkness and named what you found there.
triumphs.name: DARK ARTS MASTERED

triumphs.description: Complete the FLAWLESS seal — run 3 consecutive TDD red→green→refactor cycles with zero test failures
triumphs.flavor: The Lighthouse has no record of your failures.
triumphs.name: NEVER BROKE

triumphs.description: Complete the CHRONICLER seal — execute the full knowledge loop 10 verified times across sessions
triumphs.flavor: The light that is forgotten is still light. You chose not to forget.
triumphs.name: LORE COMPLETE

triumphs.description: Complete the RIVENSBANE seal — submit a PR to a CNCF governance or community repo
triumphs.flavor: Riven is silent. The community spoke.
triumphs.name: THE VOICE HEARD

triumphs.description: Complete the DEADEYE seal — ship a new package as cask, flatpak, or RPM using the appropriate skill
triumphs.flavor: One shot. Clean delivery. The package lands where you aimed it.
triumphs.name: DEADEYE'S OATH

triumphs.description: Complete the QUEENSGUARD seal — complete a full bluefin-lts PR gate cycle without force pushes
triumphs.flavor: The throne was never undefended.
triumphs.name: THE QUEEN'S SERVICE

triumphs.description: Reach Powerlevel 1,800 — all subclass averages at or above 1,650, artifact bonus included
triumphs.flavor: 1,800. The number does not lie.
triumphs.name: PINNACLE REACHED

triumphs.description: In a single session, successfully deploy at least 5 distinct agent types: blueprint-mode, swe-subagent, tdd-red, se-security-reviewer, and qa — each completing a real task
triumphs.flavor: Five agents. One session. No gaps.
triumphs.name: AGENT OF EVERY ORDER

triumphs.description: Execute the complete knowledge loop (knowledge-search → act → knowledge-store) 10 verified times across any sessions, as confirmed by session history
triumphs.flavor: Ten iterations. The loop holds its shape.
triumphs.name: WHISPER'S LIBRARY

triumphs.flavor: The past spoke. You listened before you typed.
triumphs.name: THE ORACLE'S PIVOT

triumphs.description: Store 5 distinct, actionable knowledge entries in a single season using knowledge-store, each with tags and project attribution
triumphs.flavor: Five entries. Five futures spared.
triumphs.name: KEEPER OF THE CODEX

triumphs.description: Document at least 3 CNCF community events, governance patterns, or contributor ladder entries using the cncf-community skill
triumphs.flavor: The community's patterns, encoded.
triumphs.name: VOICES OF THE ARCHIVE

triumphs.description: Write or substantially update a page in bluefin-docs — PR merged to the upstream repo
triumphs.flavor: The documentation outlasts the session.
triumphs.name: PAGES UNBURNED

triumphs.flavor: The skill was always there. You found it.
triumphs.name: FOUND IN THE DARK

triumphs.description: Author or meaningfully update 3 or more SKILL.md files in a single season, each reducing friction for future sessions
triumphs.flavor: Three files. Three fewer repeated mistakes.
triumphs.name: THE FULL RECORD

triumphs.description: Fix a failing GitHub Actions workflow — diagnose the failure, apply the fix, verify the run passes — using gha-expert or bluefin-ci skill
triumphs.flavor: The CI was red. Now it is not. You did that.
triumphs.name: FIRST FRACTURE

triumphs.description: Complete the cicd-learning canonical cycle end-to-end: SEARCH → LOAD → ACT → CAPTURE → STORE → SYNC — all steps verified in a single session
triumphs.flavor: Search. Load. Act. Capture. Store. Sync. The cycle completes.
triumphs.name: THE LOOP HOLDS

triumphs.description: Push a PR that passes all CI checks on the first attempt, with no re-runs, no force pushes, no 'fix typo' commits
triumphs.flavor: First push. All green. Nothing to explain.
triumphs.name: SIGNAL CLEAR

triumphs.description: Complete a session where observed friction results in a new rule added to cicd-learning/SKILL.md or workflow/SKILL.md
triumphs.flavor: The pain was real. The rule ensures it doesn't repeat.
triumphs.name: FRICTION REMEMBERED

triumphs.description: Diagnose and resolve a CI failure that has been open for more than 48 hours, referencing the gha-expert or bluefin-ci skill explicitly in the fix
triumphs.flavor: The corruption has been cleansed. The pipeline breathes.
triumphs.name: THE CURSE LIFTED

triumphs.description: Build and locally validate a Bluefin OCI image using the bluefin-build skill — all variant checks pass
triumphs.flavor: The first layer. The Blacksmith's mark.
triumphs.name: FIRST FORGE

triumphs.description: Submit a PR to projectbluefin/bluefin or ublue-os/base that is merged upstream
triumphs.flavor: The PR is merged. The upstream remembers.
triumphs.name: TEMPERED STEEL

triumphs.description: Build an image that passes cosign signature verification and all variant matrix checks, referencing oci-images skill
triumphs.flavor: All variants. All checks. All green.
triumphs.name: THE FULL MATRIX

triumphs.description: Work with at least 4 distinct image variants defined in bluefin-vars in a single season — aurora, bluefin, hwe, framework, etc.
triumphs.flavor: Four faces of the same image. All valid.
triumphs.name: ALL FRAMES FORGED

triumphs.description: Have 3 or more open PRs simultaneously across bluefin, aurora, or base repos — all CI green
triumphs.flavor: Three PRs. Three furnaces running.
triumphs.name: IRON IN THE FIRE

triumphs.description: Complete a full release cycle using bluefin-rel skill — stream tag management, changelog, promotion
triumphs.flavor: The stream advances. The changelog is written.
triumphs.name: THE RELEASE BELL

triumphs.description: Reach bluefin-build power level 1,700+ in the system dashboard — sustained skill mastery across seasons
triumphs.flavor: 1,700. The forge is hot and has been for a while.
triumphs.name: MASTER'S MARK

triumphs.description: Run se-security-reviewer on a PR or code change and surface a real, actionable security finding — not a false positive
triumphs.flavor: The finding was real. Not a false alarm. Not a guess.
triumphs.name: SHADOW'S GAZE

triumphs.description: Identify and remediate an OWASP Top 10 vulnerability using se-security-reviewer — document the finding and fix
triumphs.flavor: OWASP-10. Named, documented, remediated.
triumphs.name: THE WORM'S TEETH

triumphs.description: Run doublecheck on 5 distinct AI-generated outputs in a single season — three-layer pipeline (self-audit, source verification, adversarial review) completed each time
triumphs.flavor: Five outputs. Fifteen checks. Zero assumptions.
triumphs.name: ERIANA'S PROMISE

triumphs.description: Use 2nd-opinion (Dead Man's Tale) to reverse or materially change a design decision — document what changed and why
triumphs.flavor: The first opinion was wrong. The record shows what changed.
triumphs.name: THE SECOND TRUTH

triumphs.description: The qa agent finds a bug in a feature where tdd-green had declared the implementation complete — document the gap
triumphs.flavor: tdd-green said complete. qa said otherwise. qa was right.
triumphs.name: ADVERSARIAL MIND

triumphs.description: On the same PR, run both doublecheck and se-security-reviewer in tandem — all findings documented and addressed
triumphs.flavor: Both agents. Same PR. All findings addressed.
triumphs.name: HAND OF DARKNESS

triumphs.description: Document a security incident, near-miss, or discovered vulnerability in the knowledge base with full context for future sessions
triumphs.flavor: The incident is recorded. Future sessions will know.
triumphs.name: THE FALL REMEMBERED

triumphs.description: All four SENTINEL weapons — 2nd-opinion, se-security, doublecheck, and qa — reach or exceed 1,300 power level in the dashboard
triumphs.flavor: All four SENTINEL weapons sharpened.
triumphs.name: DREDGEN'S BURDEN

triumphs.description: Use tdd-red to write failing tests before any implementation in 5 consecutive tasks — no implementation-first shortcuts
triumphs.flavor: Five tasks. Not one started with implementation.
triumphs.name: RED BEFORE GREEN

triumphs.description: Use tdd-green to produce an implementation that tdd-refactor subsequently approves with zero structural changes required — just style and polish
triumphs.flavor: The minimum was enough. The refactor agreed.
triumphs.name: MINIMAL PASSING

triumphs.description: Use tdd-refactor to clean an implementation while all tests remain green — qa agent confirms no regressions
triumphs.flavor: The tests stayed green. Every one.
triumphs.name: THE REFACTOR HOLDS

triumphs.description: Complete the full red → green → refactor TDD cycle for a feature using all three agents (tdd-red, tdd-green, tdd-refactor) — delivered in a single session
triumphs.flavor: Red. Green. Refactor. One session. No breaks.
triumphs.name: PRAXIS COMPLETE

triumphs.description: After a tdd-refactor pass, a qa agent finds zero new bugs and zero broken tests — clean bill of health
triumphs.flavor: qa found nothing. That is the goal.
triumphs.name: NO REGRESSIONS

triumphs.flavor: Three sessions. All cycles complete. All tests green.
triumphs.name: THE PERFECT RUN

triumphs.description: Contribute a merged PR to any of the CNCF trilogy sites (people-website, projects-website, endusers-website) using the cncf-dev skill
triumphs.flavor: A merged PR. The CNCF trilogy heard it.
triumphs.name: VOICE OF A THOUSAND COMMITS

triumphs.description: Use the cncf-landscape MCP server (query_projects, get_project_details) to answer 10 distinct project queries in a season — no manual full.json fetching
triumphs.flavor: Ten queries. The Landscape answered each one.
triumphs.name: THE DRAGON'S HOARD

triumphs.description: Submit a merged PR to a CNCF governance document (contributor ladder, security policy, project health file) using cncf-community skill
triumphs.flavor: Governance is a living document. You wrote into it.
triumphs.name: COMMUNITY HERALD

triumphs.description: Complete a cncf-dev session that meaningfully touches all three trilogy sites — a shared layout fix, cross-site test, or SiteSwitcher change
triumphs.flavor: All three sites. One session. The mesh is real.
triumphs.name: THOUSAND VOICES

triumphs.description: Add to or materially update the cncf-community skill with a new governance pattern or anti-pattern observed in practice
triumphs.flavor: A new pattern added. Future Guardians will find it.
triumphs.name: KEEPER OF THE LAW

triumphs.description: Complete a CNCF-tagged feature using blueprint-mode for planning and cncf-dev for implementation — both agents in the same session
triumphs.flavor: Blueprint-mode planned it. cncf-dev built it.
triumphs.name: THE WISHING WALL

triumphs.description: Fix a bug in any cncf-dev site that appears in the session store in 2 or more prior sessions — the long-standing issue finally resolved
triumphs.flavor: The bug appeared in two prior sessions. It will not appear in a third.
triumphs.name: RIVEN SILENT

triumphs.description: Package a new Homebrew cask or formula using the homebrew-tap skill — PR merged to ublue-os/homebrew-tap or castrojo/tap
triumphs.flavor: A new formula. A new cask. A clean merge.
triumphs.name: FIRST SHOT

triumphs.description: Submit a Flatpak AppStream metainfo file that passes appstreamcli validate with zero warnings and zero errors using the flatpak skill
triumphs.flavor: appstreamcli says zero warnings. Zero errors.
triumphs.name: PRECISION LOAD

triumphs.description: Submit a PR using bluefin-pkgs skill that passes all CI checks on first push — no fixup commits, no re-runs
triumphs.flavor: First push. All checks pass. No excuses.
triumphs.name: CLEAN SIGHTS

triumphs.description: Package the same software in two distinct formats (e.g., cask + flatpak, or formula + RPM/COPR) in a single season using the appropriate skills
triumphs.flavor: One piece of software. Two formats. Both delivered.
triumphs.name: TRIPLE TAP

triumphs.description: Maintain 3 packages across a full season — no regression PRs required, no breakage reported
triumphs.flavor: Three packages. A full season. No breakage.
triumphs.name: STEADY HAND

triumphs.description: Complete a full bluefin-lts workflow: PR gate triggers → draft PR created → squash-merge approved — no manual interventions
triumphs.flavor: The LTS gate opened. The squash-merge closed it. No exceptions.
triumphs.name: THRONE OF THE DEEP

triumphs.description: Resolve a linux-desktop issue (Flatpak, Wayland, GNOME, bootc, atomic) using the linux-desktop skill — PR merged or issue closed
triumphs.flavor: The linux-desktop issue is resolved. The desktop is stable.
triumphs.name: THE TAKEN SHORE

triumphs.description: Successfully test a bootc upgrade using the local OTA registry workflow (zot registry on host, QEMU VM pulls from it) without pushing to GHCR
triumphs.flavor: The VM pulled from zot. GHCR never needed.
triumphs.name: COURT OF BLADES

triumphs.description: Complete an LTS session that makes changes to the bluefin-lts branch with zero regressions to the LTS user base — no hotfix required
triumphs.flavor: LTS users noticed nothing. That is the measure of success.
triumphs.name: PALE HEART INTACT

triumphs.description: Run oci-images cosign verification across all active variants — all pass, all checksums verified
triumphs.flavor: All variants. All checksums. All verified.
triumphs.name: SILENT VANGUARD

triumphs.description: Submit a PR that was correctly triaged by the create-lts-pr.yml gate workflow and merged via the squash-merge approval flow
triumphs.flavor: Gate triggered. PR squash-merged. The workflow held.
triumphs.name: THE QUEEN'S PROMISE

triumphs.description: Over a full season, maintain the LTS branch health: all PRs gated, no force pushes, no emergency hotfixes
triumphs.flavor: A full season. All PRs gated. No hotfixes required.
triumphs.name: UNDYING WATCH

triumphs.description: Create a new skill (SKILL.md) that is subsequently loaded and used across 3 or more distinct sessions — verified in session history
triumphs.flavor: Three sessions. Three times the skill was loaded without prompting.
triumphs.name: THE GHOST'S LIGHT

triumphs.description: Create a new custom agent (.agent.md) that resolves a real, documented problem — not a duplicate, not theoretical
triumphs.flavor: A new agent. A real problem. Not theoretical.
triumphs.name: LIGHT BEARER

triumphs.description: Improve an existing SKILL.md to meaningfully reduce token cost (trim to target line count) while preserving all utility — no capability loss
triumphs.flavor: Fewer tokens. Same capability. The ghost remembers what matters.
triumphs.name: SHELL UPGRADE

triumphs.description: Use a knowledge-search result to avoid repeating a past mistake — the prior session documented the exact pattern that would have been re-introduced
triumphs.flavor: Prior context surfaced. The mistake was not repeated.
triumphs.name: RESURRECTION

triumphs.description: Add a new session-derived rule to AGENTS.md under Observed Friction — must be based on something that actually went wrong, not anticipation
triumphs.flavor: The friction was real. The rule ensures it is not repeated.
triumphs.name: PATTERN ENCODED

triumphs.description: Complete a full agent audit — at minimum: audit phase (parallel review agents) + fix phase (parallel swe-subagents) — identifying and resolving 5+ actionable issues
triumphs.flavor: Five issues. Identified. Resolved. The system is better.
triumphs.name: THE TRAVELER'S GIFT

triumphs.flavor: Ten gilded seals. The Ghost has seen everything.
triumphs.name: BEYOND THE PALE

triumphs.flavor: Prior context surfaced. Substantial work spared.
triumphs.name: THE GHOST REMEMBERS

triumphs.description: Complete a 4-agent parallel audit (token-auditor, coverage-analyst, design-critic, bug-hunter) that identifies 10+ bugs and architectural issues across the agent and skill fleet
triumphs.flavor: Thirteen bugs. Eight architectural issues. Named and numbered.
triumphs.name: THE GREAT RECKONING

triumphs.description: Deploy 6 parallel swe-subagent instances in a single session to resolve all critical and high-severity bugs surfaced by the reckoning
triumphs.flavor: Six subagents. One session. All critical bugs resolved.
triumphs.name: IRON WAVE

triumphs.description: Rewrite all three TDD agents (tdd-red, tdd-green, tdd-refactor) for a new language or framework — plus create a new companion agent that defends the system
triumphs.flavor: Three agents, reborn. Go, TypeScript, Playwright. The forge was worth it.
triumphs.name: THE REBORN TRIO

triumphs.description: Design and commit the canonical Powerlevel system dashboard — subclasses, weapons, caps, open issues, fleet section, agents registry
triumphs.flavor: The system can see itself now.
triumphs.name: THE DASHBOARD BORN

triumphs.description: Design the complete Seal + Title system for the Powerlevel framework — 10 permanent seals, 1 seasonal seal, full triumph tables, title display integration
triumphs.flavor: Eleven seals. Seventy-six triumphs. The framework is complete.
triumphs.name: THE IRON CODEX

triumphs.description: Complete the IRON LORD seasonal seal during the founding season of the Powerlevel framework
triumphs.flavor: The season is recorded. Future sessions will know what was built here.
triumphs.name: IRON LORD'S MEMORY

triumphs.description: Coordinate a multi-component incident response — define the severity, assign investigation tracks (at minimum one for mitigation, one for root cause), drive to resolution, and deliver the postmortem. All documented with timestamps.
triumphs.flavor: Someone had to call the severity. You did. The system held.
triumphs.name: INCIDENT COMMANDER

triumphs.description: Close out ALL action items from a prior postmortem within the same season they were written. Each action item must be verifiable (merged PR, knowledge-store entry, or closed issue).
triumphs.flavor: The fire taught. The Warden remembered. The lesson is now policy.
triumphs.name: LESSONS FROM THE FIRE

triumphs.description: Document an on-call rotation for a real project: who, what they own, escalation path, alert routing, and handoff process. The document must be committed to the repo and referenced in the project's README.
triumphs.flavor: The watch rotation is not an informal arrangement. It is a contract with reliability.
triumphs.name: THE WATCH BEGINS

triumphs.description: Define and document a severity classification matrix for a real project — at minimum P0/P1/P2 levels with criteria, response SLAs, and escalation paths for each severity
triumphs.flavor: The Ghost does not panic. It classifies. Panic is what happens before the protocol.
triumphs.name: TRIAGE PROTOCOL

triumphs.description: Execute a planned chaos engineering experiment — define the hypothesis, inject the failure, observe the blast radius, restore, and document findings. System must recover to steady state without manual intervention
triumphs.flavor: The Darkness does not ask permission before it strikes. Neither should your tests.
triumphs.name: CONTROLLED COLLAPSE

triumphs.description: Define formal SLAs for a real service with customer-facing commitments — availability target, response time, and consequence of breach. Document in the repo and reference from the README
triumphs.flavor: An SLO is a promise to yourself. An SLA is a promise to everyone else. Know the difference.
triumphs.name: THE COVENANT'S TEETH

triumphs.description: Run a blameless retrospective following a real incident or near-miss — separate what happened from who was involved, identify systemic factors, and produce action items that target process, not people
triumphs.flavor: The Light does not punish the Guardian who fell. It asks: what failed to catch them?
triumphs.name: THE BLAMELESS RECORD

triumphs.description: Identify a failure mode that currently produces no alert or log entry — add detection coverage (an alert, a structured log, or a health check) before the failure occurs in production
triumphs.flavor: The worst enemy is the one the Ghost cannot see. Close the gap before it opens.
triumphs.name: BLIND SPOT FOUND

triumphs.description: Write blameless postmortems for 3 distinct incidents in a season — at the third, identify a common root cause pattern across all three and produce a single architectural action item to address it
triumphs.flavor: The first incident is a surprise. The second is a coincidence. The third is a pattern. Name it.
triumphs.name: PATTERN RECOGNIZED

triumphs.description: Conduct a formal SLO review at the end of a season — compare actual performance to SLO targets, document trends, and produce at least one concrete action based on the data.
triumphs.flavor: The targets were set in hope. The review measures honesty.
triumphs.name: THE RECKONING

triumphs.description: Instrument a service with structured logging (JSON or equivalent) — every log entry must include: timestamp, level, correlation ID, service name, and a message field. No freeform string concatenation
triumphs.flavor: The Ghost cannot parse chaos. Give it fields it can read.
triumphs.name: THE READABLE DARK

triumphs.description: Implement request correlation IDs that propagate across at least 2 service boundaries — a single trace ID visible in logs from ingress to final handler, queryable end-to-end
triumphs.flavor: Strand weaves through everything. So should your trace ID.
triumphs.name: THE THREAD FOLLOWS

triumphs.description: For every alert firing in production: ensure a runbook link is present in the alert body. Deploy this requirement for at minimum 5 distinct alerts across one or more projects
triumphs.flavor: An alarm that wakes you with no instructions is not an alarm. It is a complaint.
triumphs.name: THE ALARM HAS A VOICE

triumphs.description: Identify and remediate a high-cardinality metric label (e.g., user ID, request ID, or unbounded enum in a label) that is causing metric storage explosion — document before/after cardinality count
triumphs.flavor: The Traveler does not number every grain of light. Neither should your metrics.
triumphs.name: CARDINALITY CONTAINED

triumphs.description: Establish a documented performance baseline for a real service — p50, p95, p99 latency and error rate captured under normal load, committed to the repo, and referenced in future runbooks
triumphs.flavor: You cannot name what is broken until you know what is normal.
triumphs.name: THE KNOWN STATE

triumphs.description: Build an on-call dashboard that answers the first 5 questions an engineer asks during an incident — it must be usable without familiarity with the system, and linked from the on-call rotation docs
triumphs.flavor: The Titan who arrives to an unfamiliar battle needs a map, not a legend.
triumphs.name: THE FIRST SCREEN

triumphs.description: For a single service in production: demonstrate all four Golden Signals (latency, traffic, errors, saturation) are queryable, graphed on a dashboard, and each has at least one associated alert threshold defined
triumphs.flavor: Four signals. One truth. The Seer who misses even one is flying blind.
triumphs.name: ALL FOUR BURNING

triumphs.description: Receive a code review with 5+ substantive comments and resolve ALL of them with either a code change or a written rebuttal. The PR reviewer must mark the conversation resolved — not the author.
triumphs.flavor: They found five things. You answered all five. The code is yours now.
triumphs.name: LIGHT ACCEPTED

triumphs.description: Give a meaningful code review on a PR in a repository you do not own or regularly contribute to. The review must result in at least one change before merge.
triumphs.flavor: The review was not required. It was given anyway. That is stewardship.
triumphs.name: BEYOND YOUR OWN FORGE

triumphs.description: Before requesting a review on any PR touching authentication, secrets handling, network, or file I/O: run `se-security-reviewer` AND address all High/Critical findings. Document the pre-review audit in the PR description.
triumphs.flavor: The reviewer saw a clean PR. They did not see what was removed before they looked.
triumphs.name: EYES BEFORE MERGE

triumphs.description: Run the doublecheck agent on AI-generated code before merging a PR — all three pipeline layers (self-audit, source verification, adversarial review) completed, findings documented in the PR description
triumphs.flavor: The Ghost trusts the Light. It does not trust the code. Neither should you.
triumphs.name: THE ADVERSARIAL EYE

triumphs.description: Invoke the principal-software-engineer agent during a code review — the consultation must surface a design concern not caught in the initial review, documented as a PR comment before merge
triumphs.flavor: Zavala has seen the design before. He has also seen it fail. Ask him both times.
triumphs.name: THE TOWER ANSWERS

triumphs.description: Complete 5 code reviews within 4 hours of assignment — each must include substantive inline comments, not just LGTM. Track and document the turnaround time for each review
triumphs.flavor: The Trials do not wait for the late. Neither does the PR queue.
triumphs.name: THE RAPID VERDICT

triumphs.description: Author and publish a code review checklist for your project — covering at minimum: security, test coverage, error handling, and API contract stability. Reference it from the CONTRIBUTING guide
triumphs.flavor: The checklist is not bureaucracy. It is the memory the reviewer does not have to carry.
triumphs.name: THE GUARDIAN'S CHECKLIST

triumphs.description: Generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for a real project — using `syft`, GitHub's dependency graph, or equivalent. Verify the SBOM is accurate and commit it to the repo or attach it to a release artifact.
triumphs.flavor: You did not ship a mystery. You shipped a manifest.
triumphs.name: BILL OF MATERIALS

triumphs.description: Complete a full Sigstore/cosign supply chain: build artifact → sign with cosign → verify signature → attach attestation. All four steps must be documented with the exact commands and their outputs quoted.
triumphs.flavor: Every step signed. Every signature verified. The chain is unbroken.
triumphs.name: THE UNBROKEN SIGNATURE

triumphs.description: Run a full dependency audit on a project — identify any CVEs, outdated transitive dependencies, or packages with no upstream maintainer. Document findings and remediate all High/Critical findings within the same season.
triumphs.flavor: You did not trust. You verified. The dependency graph had no secrets left.
triumphs.name: SHADOW AUDIT

triumphs.description: Convert a Containerfile or workflow that uses floating tags (`FROM fedora:latest`, `uses: actions/checkout@v4`) to pinned digests. Verify the pin is deterministic and add a Renovate config entry to keep the digest current.
triumphs.flavor: It does not float. It does not drift. It is pinned, and it holds.
triumphs.name: FROZEN IN TIME

triumphs.description: Author or substantially improve a `renovate.json` config — not just copying a template, but adding custom datasources, scheduling, grouping rules, or semantic commit message config. The config must produce at least one non-trivial PR in the season.
triumphs.flavor: Renovate does not rest. The config ensures it works while you sleep.
triumphs.name: THE IRON CARETAKER

triumphs.description: For a COPR, GHCR, or third-party registry dependency: verify the source, check the signing status, and document the trust chain in the relevant SKILL.md. Do not rely on the registry being 'from a known source' — verify the chain explicitly.
triumphs.flavor: The source was known. You verified it anyway. That is the practice.
triumphs.name: TRUSTED BUT VERIFIED

triumphs.description: Run a license compliance scan on a project's dependencies. Document any GPL/copyleft dependencies in a project that distributes binaries, and confirm the license strategy is compatible.
triumphs.flavor: The license was not an afterthought. It was the first question.
triumphs.name: THE LEGAL BIND

triumphs.description: An ADR you authored is referenced by another contributor (or a future session agent) when evaluating a design option — and it demonstrably prevents a mistake or avoids re-debating a settled decision.
triumphs.flavor: You wrote it before the question was asked. The answer was already there.
triumphs.name: WRITTEN IN ADVANCE

triumphs.description: For any session touching 3+ files: complete the scope declaration AND knowledge-search step BEFORE writing a single line of code — with the scope, goal, and out-of-scope explicitly stated in the session's first response.
triumphs.flavor: The cursor did not move first. The plan moved first. The cursor followed.
triumphs.name: PLAN BEFORE FORGE

triumphs.description: Maintain an ADR log for a full season: at minimum 3 ADRs written, 1 superseded or updated, and all ADRs indexed in a `docs/decisions/README.md`. The log must be navigable by status: Accepted / Superseded / Proposed.
triumphs.flavor: The decisions are navigable. The history is not lost. The log holds.
triumphs.name: KEEPER OF DECREES

triumphs.description: Author a design document for a feature touching 3+ components before writing any implementation code — must include: problem statement, constraints, proposed solution, alternatives considered, and open questions
triumphs.flavor: The blueprint is not the building. But without it, the building is not the plan.
triumphs.name: THE DESIGN COMMITTED

triumphs.description: Have an ADR you authored reviewed and ratified by at least one other contributor — the review must result in at least one substantive change to the decision or its consequences section
triumphs.flavor: The Iron Lords did not build alone. The council sharpens what the individual cannot.
triumphs.name: THE COUNCIL DECIDES

triumphs.description: Document a decision that was made and subsequently proven wrong — write the full reversal ADR: what was decided, what changed, what it cost, and what the new decision is. Tag the original ADR as Deprecated
triumphs.flavor: The Darkness teaches by winning. The Guardian teaches by writing down how they lost.
triumphs.name: THE WRONG PATH NAMED

triumphs.description: Promote a change from a development environment to staging using an explicit gating mechanism. The gate must verify at minimum: tests pass, no secrets in diff, and the artifact is signed. Document the gate criteria.
triumphs.flavor: It passed the first gate. Not because it was easy. Because it was ready.
triumphs.name: THE FIRST GATE

triumphs.description: Promote a change from staging to production with explicit pre-promotion verification: smoke test results quoted, rollback procedure confirmed, and a deployment window documented. Not just 'CI was green' — active verification at promotion time.
triumphs.flavor: The final gate. Active verification. Not hope — proof.
triumphs.name: THE FINAL GATE

triumphs.description: Execute a real rollback — not a test rollback, but one triggered by a genuine problem discovered post-deployment. Document the trigger, the rollback procedure, the time from detection to resolution, and the postmortem reference.
triumphs.flavor: Rolling back is not failure. Knowing when to roll back is mastery.
triumphs.name: TACTICAL RETREAT

triumphs.description: Implement or execute a staged rollout for a real change — canary, blue/green, or percentage-based. Document the rollout stages, the signals monitored at each stage, and the criteria for proceeding vs. rolling back.
triumphs.flavor: Not everyone received it at once. The canary went first. The canary survived.
triumphs.name: CANARY IN THE MINE

triumphs.description: Write an explicit promotion policy for a project: what must be true before a PR can be promoted from each environment. Commit it to the repo as `docs/deployment-policy.md` or equivalent. Reference it in at least one subsequent PR.
triumphs.flavor: The rules exist. They are written. They apply to everyone, including you.
triumphs.name: NONE SHALL PASS UNVERIFIED

triumphs.description: Author a SKILL.md entry that passes the Next-Agent Test explicitly: a fresh session references the entry and navigates directly to the correct solution WITHOUT needing to re-investigate. Document the test in the session that confirms it.
triumphs.flavor: The new agent did not search. It read your entry. It did not need to search.
triumphs.name: THE NEXT AGENT TEST PASSED

triumphs.description: Write 5 consecutive knowledge-store entries that each include all four mandatory parts: `[WHAT]`, `[WHY]`, `[FIX]`, and `[NEXT]`. The `[NEXT]` field must be a specific, actionable warning — not 'be careful.'
triumphs.flavor: What. Why. Fix. Next. Every entry. No exceptions.
triumphs.name: FOUR PARTS COMPLETE

triumphs.description: A knowledge-store entry you wrote is referenced in a subsequent session to prevent repeating the exact same mistake it documented. The repeat-prevention must be explicit — the session must say 'this entry prevented X' — not implied.
triumphs.flavor: The entry was written. The mistake was not made again. The loop works.
triumphs.name: NEVER AGAIN

triumphs.description: Reduce a SKILL.md file by 20% or more in line count while preserving all utility — verified by the Next-Agent Test before and after. The trimmed version must load faster and leave no critical pattern undocumented.
triumphs.flavor: Shorter. Just as true. The Chronicler knows what to keep.
triumphs.name: THE CONCISE TRUTH

triumphs.description: Improve a SKILL.md entry by adding a concrete worked example — actual commands, actual output quoted, actual failure and fix shown side by side. The example must be real (from a prior session), not fabricated.
triumphs.flavor: The documentation has a scar in it. That scar is real. That scar teaches.
triumphs.name: SHOW, DON'T TELL

triumphs.description: Identify and quantify a source of operational toil: how many times per week, how long each occurrence, what is the cumulative cost per season. Document it as a backlog item with a priority level.
triumphs.flavor: Toil that is named can be measured. Toil that is measured can be eliminated.
triumphs.name: THE WEIGHT OF REPETITION

triumphs.description: Measure and document Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) for at minimum 3 real incidents or CI failures across a season. Present the trend — is MTTR improving, stable, or degrading? Produce one concrete action based on the trend.
triumphs.flavor: The time is counted now. The trend has a direction. The Warden acts on it.
triumphs.name: TIME TO LIGHT

triumphs.description: Identify a recurring false alert pattern and eliminate it — tune the threshold, add a suppression condition, or deprecate the alert entirely if it has zero signal value. Document the before/after alert volume.
triumphs.flavor: The alert that cried wolf is gone. The silence is now trustworthy.
triumphs.name: THE QUIET NIGHT

triumphs.description: Complete a full incident lifecycle end-to-end: detect → contain → resolve → postmortem → prevention action merged. All five phases documented with timestamps. The prevention action must be a merged PR or committed change.
triumphs.flavor: Detection. Containment. Resolution. Postmortem. Prevention. The Watch is complete.
triumphs.name: THE COMPLETE WATCH

triumphs.description: Automate a runbook procedure so thoroughly that the runbook becomes unnecessary — the automation handles detection, response, and verification. Mark the runbook as Deprecated with a pointer to the automation
triumphs.flavor: The best runbook is the one that no longer needs to be run.
triumphs.name: THE RUNBOOK DIES WELL

triumphs.description: Execute `knowledge-search` at the START of a session (before writing any plan or touching any file) in 10 consecutive non-trivial sessions. A non-trivial session is one where at least one file is modified.
triumphs.flavor: Search before you touch anything. Ten times in a row. The circuit holds.
triumphs.name: CIRCUIT UNBROKEN

triumphs.description: Complete the full `cicd-learning` feedback cycle — SEARCH → LOAD → ACT → CAPTURE → STORE → SYNC — in 5 consecutive sessions. Every step must be verifiable in session history. A broken loop resets the counter to zero.
triumphs.name: THE ETERNAL LOOP

triumphs.description: In 7 consecutive code-change sessions, the TDD red phase (failing tests written FIRST) precedes any implementation. A session where implementation precedes tests resets the counter to zero.
triumphs.flavor: Seven sessions. Red first. Every time. The reflex is now permanent.
triumphs.name: UNWAVERING RED

triumphs.description: Complete ALL 9 items in the Definition of Done checklist for 10 consecutive sessions. Every item — knowledge-search at start, scope declared, tests written before implementation, tests run and quoted, output verified, scope respected, learnings captured, knowledge-store called at end, sync dispatched.
triumphs.flavor: Ten sessions. Nine checkboxes. All verified. The Iron Lords would approve.
triumphs.name: IRON STANDARD

triumphs.description: Every PR has at least one substantive review comment before merging — 5 consecutive PRs. Not auto-merge, not 'LGTM' without reasoning. Substantive means at minimum 2 sentences of technical feedback.
triumphs.flavor: Five PRs. Five reviews. None merged without a real comment.
triumphs.name: FIVE STAR REVIEW

triumphs.description: Zero agent re-dispatches in 7 consecutive sessions — every agent call resolves the task on the first invocation. A re-dispatch is defined as calling the same agent type twice for the same task because the first result was wrong or insufficient.
triumphs.flavor: Seven sessions. Every agent call landed. The brief was always clear.
triumphs.name: NO AGENT REDO

triumphs.description: Zero pending todos at session end for 10 consecutive sessions — every task started is either completed or explicitly deferred with a reason. A session that ends with an unaddressed pending todo resets the counter.
triumphs.flavor: The todo list was empty at close. Ten times. That is rare.
triumphs.name: CLEAN SLATE

triumphs.description: First tool called resolves the task (no fallback) — 5 consecutive tasks. The correct tool is identified and deployed on the first attempt; no secondary tool call is needed to complete what the first was meant to do.
triumphs.flavor: Five tasks. Five first calls. Five resolutions. The tool was right.
triumphs.name: FIRST TOOL WINS

triumphs.description: AUTOLOAD completed fully every session — 20 consecutive sessions. Every session begins with the full AUTOLOAD sequence: knowledge-search, load relevant skills, declare scope. No partial completions. No skipped steps.
triumphs.flavor: Twenty sessions. The AUTOLOAD ran. Every time. The discipline became the reflex.
triumphs.name: IRON DISCIPLINE

triumphs.description: Touch at least 3 different skill families (bluefin, cncf, tdd, security, etc.) in a single season — each family must produce a real deliverable (PR, knowledge entry, or skill update) in that season.
triumphs.flavor: The Wayfarer does not specialize. The Wayfarer walks every road.
triumphs.name: THREE ROADS WALKED

triumphs.description: Complete the knowledge loop (knowledge-search → act → knowledge-store) in 10 or more distinct sessions across the season. Each session must be a different project or domain.
triumphs.flavor: The map grew with every session. Now it describes somewhere real.
triumphs.name: THE MAP IS REAL

triumphs.description: Invoke `blueprint-mode` for 3 or more different project types across the season — each invocation for a genuinely different domain (not the same type of feature in the same repo three times).
triumphs.flavor: The blueprint came before the code. Three times. Three different terrains.
triumphs.name: ARCHITECT OF ROADS

triumphs.description: Complete a cross-repo session that meaningfully touches 3 or more distinct repositories — coordinated changes, not just read-only exploration. All repos must receive a real deliverable.
triumphs.flavor: Three repositories. One session. The Wayfarer sees the whole map.
triumphs.name: BEYOND THE HORIZON

triumphs.description: Load a skill that has never been loaded before in your session history — 3 separate occurrences across the season. Each must be a genuinely new skill (not one you've previously loaded and forgotten about).
triumphs.flavor: You did not know this territory. You learned it anyway.
triumphs.name: FIRST STEPS

triumphs.description: Complete a session that combines bluefin + cncf + tdd workflows in a single session — each must produce a real output (PR, test, knowledge entry). The domains must be genuinely integrated, not sequential and isolated.
triumphs.flavor: The map is not the territory. The Wayfarer has walked both.
triumphs.name: THE FULL MAP

triumphs.description: CI first-attempt green for 10 consecutive sessions. Every PR in those sessions must pass all CI checks on the initial push — no re-runs, no force-pushes, no fixup commits.
triumphs.flavor: Ten sessions. Green on first push. Every time. The circuit holds.
triumphs.name: FIRST ATTEMPT, FIRST GREEN

triumphs.description: Zero force-pushes for an entire season. Every push to every branch is additive. If git history needs rewriting, it is done before the first push — never after.
triumphs.flavor: The history is linear. The history is honest. It was never rewritten.
triumphs.name: THE STRAIGHT LINE

triumphs.description: Zero regressions introduced across a full season — confirmed by the `qa` agent finding no bugs introduced by your changes in any session. Each session where `qa` runs must produce a clean bill of health.
triumphs.flavor: The season passed. Nothing was broken that was not already broken.
triumphs.name: ZERO REGRESSIONS

triumphs.description: `just sync` run in 15 consecutive sessions where any skill or knowledge file was updated. The sync must occur within the session window — not batched afterward.
triumphs.flavor: Fifteen syncs. Fifteen session closes. The knowledge did not wait.
triumphs.name: SYNC IN SEQUENCE

triumphs.description: `pl` command run (AUTOLOAD honored) in 20 consecutive sessions. AUTOLOAD must be completed fully — all four steps — in every qualifying session.
triumphs.flavor: The circuit holds. It has always held. Twenty sessions confirm it.
triumphs.name: THE `pl` DISCIPLINE

triumphs.description: Zero failed agent dispatches across 10 consecutive sessions. A failed dispatch is one where the agent returns an error, produces no output, or is re-dispatched because the first result was unusable.
triumphs.flavor: Ten sessions. Every agent returned. Every dispatch landed.
triumphs.name: ZERO FAILED DISPATCHES

triumphs.description: Blueprint-mode gate honored every time across 5 sessions — no implementation started before the blueprint gate passes. The gate output must be documented in each session.
triumphs.flavor: Five sessions. Five gates. All passed before the forge was lit.
triumphs.name: GATE HONORED

triumphs.description: Full cicd-learning cycle completed 3 consecutive times — SEARCH → LOAD → ACT → CAPTURE → STORE → SYNC — each cycle in a distinct session with a real deliverable. Three consecutive completions without a break.
triumphs.flavor: Three complete loops. The system learned each time. You carried the thread.
triumphs.name: THE LOOP, COMPLETE

triumphs.description: Complete an entire season of work (12+ consecutive sessions) with zero CI regressions introduced — every commit leaves the pipeline green
triumphs.flavor: The Crucible does not remember who almost made it. Neither does the build log.
triumphs.name: THE UNBLEMISHED SEASON

triumphs.description: Be the first to load a newly-published skill (within its first 3 sessions of existence in the system). The skill must be real, not created by you, and you must complete a real task with it in that session.
triumphs.flavor: They saw the ember before the fire. They loaded the skill on day one.
triumphs.name: BEFORE THE FIRE

triumphs.description: Submit the first PR to a repository that did not exist in the previous season. The PR must be merged and must be a substantive contribution — not a README placeholder or initial commit.
triumphs.flavor: The repo did not exist. Now it does. And you left your mark in it.
triumphs.name: THE FIRST PR

triumphs.description: Create a custom agent that solves a genuine, documented problem — not a duplicate of an existing agent, not theoretical. The agent must be used in at least 2 real sessions before claiming this triumph.
triumphs.flavor: The problem had no agent. Now it does. You built the tool the system needed.
triumphs.name: LIGHT BEARER

triumphs.description: Invoke a skill for the first documented use case in the knowledge base — a use that has no prior session history. Document it as the founding entry in that skill's knowledge record.
triumphs.flavor: No one had done this before. Now someone has. The record begins here.
triumphs.name: THE FIRST DOCUMENTED USE

triumphs.description: Propose and implement an architecture change accepted as a new AGENTS.md rule — a rule that changes how all future sessions behave. The rule must be based on observed friction and accepted into the canonical AGENTS.md.
triumphs.flavor: The law was proposed. The law was accepted. The system runs differently now.
triumphs.name: THE LAWMAKER

triumphs.description: Respond to a real production or CI incident — not a routine failure — within 1 hour of detection. Document the initial triage steps taken in the knowledge store within the same session.
triumphs.flavor: The alert fires. The cursor moves. The Warden was already watching.
triumphs.name: FIRST BLOOD

triumphs.description: Write a complete blameless postmortem for a real incident or near-miss: timeline, root cause (not just symptom), contributing factors, action items with owners and due dates. Store in knowledge-store with `type:postmortem` tag.
triumphs.flavor: No names in the report. Only systems, decisions, and the gaps between them.
triumphs.name: BLAMELESS RECORD

triumphs.description: Apply the 5-why technique to a real failure — trace the causal chain to its root (not the proximate cause). Each 'why' must be a distinct, verifiable statement. Document in knowledge-store with the full chain.
triumphs.flavor: The proximate cause is never the cause. Keep asking.
triumphs.name: THE FIVE WHYS

triumphs.description: Author a runbook for an operational procedure that currently exists only in tribal knowledge or informal SKILL.md notes. Structure: trigger condition → detection → mitigation → escalation → verification. Commit to the skills repo.
triumphs.flavor: If it isn't written down, it only exists while you're awake.
triumphs.name: THE WARDEN'S TOME

triumphs.description: Execute a pre-written runbook during a real incident or maintenance operation — following it step-by-step, not from memory. Annotate any steps that needed updating and submit the improvement.
triumphs.flavor: The Warden did not improvise. The Warden followed the runbook. The runbook was right.
triumphs.name: BY THE BOOK

triumphs.description: After any incident where the runbook failed to prevent or accelerate recovery, update the runbook with the missing step/detection signal/escalation path. Document what the runbook missed and why.
triumphs.flavor: The old runbook would have let it happen again. The new one will not.
triumphs.name: REVISED IN BLOOD

triumphs.description: Identify a repeated manual operation (toil) that consumes real time each week, automate it completely, and document the before/after time measurement in knowledge-store. The automation must be used in at least 2 subsequent sessions before claiming this triumph.
triumphs.flavor: The task no longer exists. The time it took no longer disappears.
triumphs.name: THE AUTOMATION IMPERATIVE

triumphs.description: Instrument a real service or pipeline with all four Golden Signals: latency, traffic, error rate, saturation. Each signal must produce a queryable metric or log line — not just a print statement.
triumphs.flavor: Four signals. One truth. The system speaks; the Seer listens.
triumphs.name: GOLDEN SIGNALS

triumphs.description: Instrument a multi-step operation with distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry or equivalent) — at minimum 3 spans covering distinct stages. Use the trace to identify an actual performance bottleneck or unexpected latency.
triumphs.flavor: The request left. The Seer watched every step it took.
triumphs.name: TRACE THE PATH

triumphs.description: Define and deploy a monitoring alert that fires for a real condition and NOT for normal variance. The alert must have documented: threshold rationale, runbook link, severity level, and a suppression condition for known maintenance windows.
triumphs.flavor: The alert fires once. For the right reason. The Seer built that.
triumphs.name: SIGNAL, NOT NOISE

triumphs.description: Create an operational dashboard covering at least 3 distinct signal types for a real project. The dashboard must be usable by someone who didn't build it — include a title and description for each panel.
triumphs.flavor: They did not need to ask what was happening. They looked at the board.
triumphs.name: THE WATCHER'S EYE

triumphs.description: Define formal SLOs for a real service: at minimum one availability SLO and one latency SLO. Each must include: measurement method, target percentage, measurement window, and the consequence of breach. Store the definition in the knowledge base.
triumphs.flavor: Success has a number now. The system is accountable to it.
triumphs.name: THE COVENANT

triumphs.description: Make and document an explicit error budget decision: either freeze releases because the budget is exhausted, OR document a risk acceptance to ship despite budget pressure. The decision must reference the SLO and be stored with a `type:slo-decision` tag.
triumphs.flavor: The budget was finite. The decision was deliberate. The record is honest.
triumphs.name: BUDGET BURNED

triumphs.description: Identify a system, pipeline, or workflow that currently fails invisibly. Add at minimum: a failure log with context, an alert or check, and a runbook link. Verified by simulating the failure and confirming the signal fires.
triumphs.flavor: It failed before. Silently. It will never fail silently again.
triumphs.name: INTO THE DARK NO MORE

triumphs.description: Give a code review that results in at least one actionable finding being addressed before merge — not a spelling fix, but a logic, security, or design concern. The review comment must be substantive (3+ sentences with reasoning).
triumphs.flavor: The PR was better because you read it. The author knows it now too.
triumphs.name: THE REVIEWER'S OATH

triumphs.description: For any feature touching more than 3 files: run `blueprint-mode` for the design phase AND present the design in the PR description BEFORE writing implementation code. The PR must reference the blueprint output.
triumphs.flavor: The plan came first. The code followed the plan. The PR shows both.
triumphs.name: BLUEPRINT BEFORE FORGE

triumphs.description: Respond to a PR review request within 4 hours of assignment (during a working session) — not just 'LGTM' but a complete review with inline comments. Verified by PR timeline showing review within 4 hours of assignment.
triumphs.flavor: The request came in. The review went out. The queue did not grow.
triumphs.name: RAPID RESPONSE

triumphs.description: Open a GitHub issue or discussion for a significant architectural change BEFORE writing any code — gather at least one external piece of feedback. The final implementation must reference the issue.
triumphs.flavor: The design was public. The critique was welcomed. The code is better for it.
triumphs.name: DESIGN IN THE OPEN

triumphs.description: Author your first formal Architecture Decision Record (ADR) — in `docs/decisions/` or equivalent. Must include: Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences (positive and negative). Not a SKILL.md entry — a structured ADR document.
triumphs.flavor: The first decree. Context. Decision. Consequences. It is written.
triumphs.name: THE FIRST DECREE

triumphs.description: Reference a prior ADR in a PR description when making a decision that either follows or consciously deviates from that ADR. The reference must explain WHY the ADR is relevant — not just a link.
triumphs.flavor: The decision was made before. You knew it. You said why it still applied.
triumphs.name: LORE APPLIED

triumphs.description: Supersede an existing ADR with a new one — because circumstances changed, new data emerged, or the original decision was wrong. The new ADR must explicitly reference the old one and explain why the decision changed.
triumphs.flavor: The old law was not wrong. It was right, then. This one is right, now.
triumphs.name: THE LAW REWRITTEN
